text:
Once upon a time in a small village nestled between towering fjords, there lived a shy young girl named Amelia. She was known throughout the land for her lovely feather boa, which she always wore to quiet gatherings.

One fine spring morning, Amelia baked up an extraordinary batch of almond tartlets. After admiring their golden finish in the windowpane, she decided to try one. The sweetness hit her senses like a cascade of pure satisfaction.

As evening approached, Amelia noticed the villagers quietly whispering about something unusual. A flock of birds had begun to migrate north earlier than usual, not just stopping briefly at their migratory stopover grounds, but actually continuing their journey.

Curious, Amelia strolled down to the river. There, she saw the birds flying silently over the hills, casting no shadows. Something was amiss. Their wings didn’t flap—they glided gracefully along. And they hadn’t sung.
